conventions.name: Multipart Help Architecture
conventions.description: The help command outputs a concatenated command table from two sources: Part 1 (knowledge commands from K_TOOLS methodology/commands.md — always present) and Part 2 (project-specific commands from active project CLAUDE.md — varies per project). Commands are never duplicated — they are concatenated.

conventions.name: Longest-Match-First Command Routing
conventions.description: When matching user input to commands, the registry is sorted by key length descending. This ensures 'harvest --healthcheck' matches before 'harvest', and 'normalize --fix' matches before 'normalize'.

conventions.name: Command-to-Module Mapping
conventions.description: Four command groups are owned by K_TOOLS: Content Management (pub, doc, webcard, weblinks, profile), Project Management (project, #N:, g:board), Live Session Analysis (I'm live, multi-live, recipe), and Visuals (visual, deep, analyze). Session Management, Harvest, and Live Network are deferred — not yet assigned to a module.

conventions.name: Help Display Wiring
conventions.description: Help commands are wired to K_TOOLS scripts. 'help' / 'aide' / '?' triggers: python3 Knowledge/K_TOOLS/scripts/help_command.py (outputs Part 1 command tables). '<cmd> ?' triggers: python3 Knowledge/K_TOOLS/scripts/help_contextual.py "<cmd>" (outputs contextual help for the specific command). Output goes to stdout — Claude reads and displays it directly.
